title: An Interview with Arm CEO Rene Haas About Selling Chips (Stratechery Interview 3-26-2026)
rawText:
An interview with Arm CEO Rene Haas about the company's decision to not just license IP but make their own chips.

An Interview with Arm CEO Rene Haas About Selling Chips
*******************************************************

( https://stratechery.com/2026/an-interview-with-arm-ceo-rene-haas-about-selling-chips/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..REzMh8HR_jCabQSEzzcAoDj5K-XYiC4P5sNF0VhiYfnIgaeQUaSdANIC0RhLJ_C9nHtySIARB6WU15UHueVhFG-MhWQwzrK6l3qKLAo8inKTgCPnYdBR35ZHb54QKKIlgfms2C1Y0NiQw01fEHE8_spWsuT-FEO-ICv7ftJR0O7aYhfaAKCe0GsRh-ck4rKx0-9wPYjL4S1XwJRjIH-hUquVed8catNd7ICx_xUjkkP5qq6q68GuYzXJRFQQg5_ZpYQM2mW4s-e-yI5_PV6I2rh--VbuqT39ejCCwq0WDp3GkqeC5Dlp8CO5AbkqPzNIXXs4DjZZm-_T1opd4zVzHA ) Thursday, March 26, 2026

Good morning,

This week’s Stratechery Interview is with Arm CEO Rene Haas, who I previously spoke to in January 2024 ( https://stratechery.com/2024/an-interview-with-arm-ceo-rene-haas/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..FcirHvjTkTpQFa9Ea_LSL13kqriRGF-nYQDa4Ip7btw6Wy69vCwFe_Eci4zymB-0asrkwwkpivx8zuh90t86gah4cTr6hJAqN4ye5yr9RDIVCaH-Wp_Ejrbv_0HXhv2jl7cZ6r5i4fa96bnOH8XIhUklhMUawoPPeFFfJCiipZBg2K4gPjKjQJMO4RXHQBSSRerD-LCyRnXj_kHmzD81VO1qNmCjc72B4bJSXR74XdUZ1Ly-_vcdhcLwGftDvI5DRQU94EKH7nxjSLGudc7NwdzLuStzxVUyUW4ywIF2pFn8OGHKfRrRMA6bz8PIYrn7MwKT-IlxjqRD2-hslr3-9w ) , and who recently made a major announcement at Arm’s first-ever standalone keynote ( https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hMWJqfw9cQo ) : the long-time IP-licensing company is undergoing a dramatic shift in its business model and selling its own chips for the first time.

We dive deep into that decision in this interview, including the meta of the keynote, Arm’s history, and how the company has evolved, particularly under Haas’ leadership. Then we get into why CPUs matter for AI, and how Arm’s CPU compares to Nvidia’s, x86, and other custom Arm silicon. At the end we discuss the risks Arm faces, including a maxed-out supply chain, and how the company will need to change to support this new direction.

As a reminder, all Stratechery content, including interviews, is available as a podcast; click the link at the top of this email to add Stratechery to your podcast player.
